pattern: Order the characters by descending attack, breaking ties by ascending defense, then sweep once keeping the best defense seen so far - anything weaker than that running best is dominated by an earlier, strictly stronger character.
hints: A character is weak only when some other character beats it on BOTH stats strictly, so equal attack or equal defense never counts as dominance.
Sort by attack descending; sorting ties by defense ascending keeps same-attack characters from falsely dominating each other while scanning.
Sweep the sorted list keeping the maximum defense seen among strictly stronger-attack characters so far; a character is weak exactly when its own defense is lower than that running maximum.

edge_inputs.description: no character is dominated on both stats
a middling character dominated by the strongest
minimum size, one dominates the other
identical attack and defense never counts as weak
tied attack with different defense is not dominance
tied defense with different attack is not dominance
a strict chain where the top character dominates both below it
extreme stat values at both ends of the range

stress.description: large random rosters near the max size
